title: Racosperma argutifolium
taxonomy: Racosperma argutifolium was previously classified in the genus Acacia. However, Racosperma is now considered an outdated name in botanical nomenclature. It has since been reclassified under the genus Vachellia. Vachellia is a genus of flowering plants in the pea family (Fabaceae). The species is native to Australia and is commonly known as the prickly acacia.
uses: Racosperma argutifolium, also known as Hakea, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Australia. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. In traditional medicine, the plant's leaves and stems are used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal problems.
